methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series D → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.5x) to $1.0B last post money.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 5 same-sector + same-stage peers in AI & Software. Range: $2.0B - $8.6B.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in AI & Compute

private.name: Wayve
private.rationale: Same sector (AI & Software), same stage (Series D), post-money $8.6B

private.name: Cohere
private.rationale: Same sector (AI & Software), same stage (Series D), post-money $7B

private.name: Hugging Face
private.rationale: Same sector (AI & Software), same stage (Series D), post-money $4.5B

private.name: ElevenLabs
private.rationale: Same sector (AI & Software), same stage (Series D), post-money $3B+

private.name: Hive AI
private.rationale: Same sector (AI & Software), same stage (Series D), post-money $2B

data.narrative:
Labelbox is a Series D AI & Software company founded by Manu Sharma, having raised $189M to date against a current post-money valuation exceeding $1.0B. The valuation analysis draws from a comp universe of 9 private peers in the same sector and stage—including Wayve ($8.6B), Cohere ($7.0B), Hugging Face ($4.5B), ElevenLabs ($3.0B), and Hive AI ($2.0B)—plus one public comparable.

Two triangulation methods apply here. Stage-progression analysis, anchoring to the $1.0B last post-money valuation and applying a typical 1.5x Series D step-up multiplier, yields $1.5B. Peer-median analysis across the five core comps produces $4.5B, with the broader peer set ranging from $2.0B to $8.6B. The dispersion reflects heterogeneity in business model, revenue, and growth profile within frontier AI.

Synthesizing these methods produces a triangulated range of $1.3B (low) to $5.2B (high), with a median of $3.0B. The wide spread reflects material uncertainty around how Labelbox's data-infrastructure positioning and unit economics compare to peers spanning autonomous driving (Wayve), large language models (Cohere, Hugging Face), and synthetic media (ElevenLabs). Resolution of this range depends critically on revenue visibility, customer concentration, and near-term path to profitability relative to the peer cohort.
